title: Algorithms & Sequencing
subtitle: step by step thinking!
overview: This session introduces students to algorithms and sequencing using fun real-life examples and activities. Students learn how computers follow step-by-step instructions to complete tasks correctly.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — classroom activities, students arranging steps, coding games, whiteboard logic diagrams.

gallery.caption: Students arranging steps in correct order

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of flowchart explanation on board
gallery.caption: Understanding flowcharts and sequencing

keyTopics: What is an Algorithm? — Step-by-step instructions
Real-life Algorithms — Brushing teeth, making tea, school routine

activity.title: Make a Sandwich Algorithm Activity
steps: Write the steps to make a sandwich
Fix mistakes and improve the algorithm

options: Step-by-step instructions to solve a problem
quiz.explanation: An algorithm is a set of step-by-step instructions used to complete a task or solve a problem.

options: Because computers need correct order of instructions
quiz.explanation: Computers follow instructions exactly in the order given.

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of an algorithm?
quiz.explanation: Making tea involves following a sequence of steps, making it an algorithm.

quiz.q: What happens if steps are in the wrong order?
quiz.explanation: Wrong sequencing can cause errors or incorrect results.
A flowchart visually represents steps in an algorithm.
Computers do exactly what we tell them through code and instructions.
Debugging means identifying and correcting errors.
Many daily life activities require correct sequencing.

quiz.q: What will happen if a robot gets unclear instructions?
options: It may do the wrong task
quiz.explanation: Robots and computers need clear instructions.

quiz.q: Which comes FIRST while brushing teeth?
quiz.explanation: Applying toothpaste is one of the first steps.
Sequence means arranging actions in proper order.
Traffic lights work in a fixed sequence: Red → Yellow → Green.

quiz.q: Which skill improves by learning algorithms?
quiz.explanation: Algorithms help improve many thinking and problem-solving skills.

quiz.q: What should we do after finding a mistake in steps?
quiz.explanation: Mistakes should be corrected through debugging.

quiz.q: Which is easier for a computer to understand?
quiz.explanation: Computers work best with clear instructions in correct order.
